questions.stem_en: At an uncontrolled intersection where two vehicles arrive at the same time, who has the right-of-way?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在無交通控制的路口，兩輛車同時到達時，誰有優先通行權？

options.text_en: The vehicle on the left
The vehicle on the right
The larger vehicle
The vehicle going faster

questions.explanation_en: At an uncontrolled intersection, if two vehicles arrive at the same time, yield to the vehicle on your right.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在無交通控制的路口，如果兩輛車同時到達，讓右側車輛先行。

questions.stem_en: When making a left turn at an intersection, you must yield to:
questions.stem_zhHant: 在路口左轉時，您必須讓哪些對象先行？

options.text_en: Only pedestrians
Only vehicles turning right
Oncoming traffic and pedestrians
No one if you have a green light
options.text_zhHant: 如果是綠燈則不需要讓任何人

questions.explanation_en: When making a left turn, you must yield to oncoming traffic and pedestrians until it is safe to complete the turn.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在路口左轉時，必須讓對向直行車輛和行人先行，直到確認安全後才能完成左轉。

questions.stem_en: When entering a road from a driveway, you must:
questions.stem_zhHant: 從車道入口進入公共道路時，您必須：

options.text_en: Honk your horn and proceed
Yield only to traffic on the left
Stop and yield to all traffic and pedestrians
options.text_zhHant: 停車並讓所有車輛和行人先行

options.text_en: Proceed if you can merge safely
options.text_zhHant: 如果能安全匯入則繼續行駛

questions.explanation_en: When entering a road from a driveway, alley, or private road, you must stop and yield to all traffic on the road and pedestrians on the sidewalk.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 從車道入口、小巷或私人道路進入公共道路時，必須停車並讓公路上所有車輛和人行道上的行人先行。

questions.stem_en: At a T-intersection without signs or signals, who has the right-of-way?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在沒有標誌或信號燈的丁字路口，誰有優先通行權？

options.text_en: Vehicles on the terminating road
options.text_zhHant: 從終止道路駛來的車輛

options.text_en: Vehicles on the through (continuing) road
The vehicle on the right regardless of which road
options.text_zhHant: 不論在哪條路上，右側車輛

options.text_en: Whoever arrives first

questions.explanation_en: At a T-intersection without signs or signals, vehicles on the through road have the right-of-way over vehicles on the terminating road.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在沒有標誌或信號燈的丁字路口，直行道路上的車輛優先於從終止道路駛來的車輛。

questions.stem_en: When entering a freeway via an on-ramp, you should:
questions.stem_zhHant: 通過匝道進入高速公路時，您應該：

options.text_en: Stop at the end of the on-ramp and wait for a gap
options.text_zhHant: 在匝道末端停車等待間隙

options.text_en: Merge at or near the speed of freeway traffic
options.text_zhHant: 以接近高速公路車流的速度匯入

options.text_en: Drive as slow as possible to be safe
options.text_zhHant: 盡可能慢速行駛以確保安全

options.text_en: Signal freeway traffic to make room for you
options.text_zhHant: 示意高速車流為你讓出空間

questions.explanation_en: When entering a freeway, you must yield to traffic already on the freeway and merge at or near their speed.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 進入高速公路時，必須讓已在高速上行駛的車輛先行，應以接近高速公路車流的速度匯入。

questions.stem_en: At a 4-way stop, if two vehicles stop at the same time, the right-of-way goes to:
questions.stem_zhHant: 在四向停車路口，如果兩輛車同時停車，優先通行權歸：

options.text_en: The vehicle on the left
The vehicle going straight
The vehicle on the right
The vehicle that honks first

questions.explanation_en: At a 4-way stop, if two vehicles stop at the same time, the vehicle on the right goes first.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在四向停車路口，如果兩輛車同時停車，右側車輛優先。

questions.stem_en: Pedestrians have the right-of-way at:
questions.stem_zhHant: 行人在以下哪些地方有優先通行權？

options.text_en: Marked crosswalks only
Both marked and unmarked crosswalks
options.text_zhHant: 有標記和無標記的人行橫道

options.text_en: Only when they have a WALK signal
Nowhere unless there is a crossing guard
options.text_zhHant: 除非有交通協管員，否則沒有

questions.explanation_en: Pedestrians have the right-of-way at both marked and unmarked crosswalks.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 行人在有標記和無標記的人行橫道上都有優先通行權。

questions.stem_en: If a pedestrian is crossing the road illegally (jaywalking), you must:
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果行人違規橫穿馬路（亂穿馬路），您必須：

options.text_en: Continue driving since they are breaking the law
options.text_zhHant: 繼續行駛因為他們在違法

options.text_en: Honk and drive around them
Still exercise due care and yield to avoid a collision
options.text_zhHant: 仍須盡合理注意並讓行以避免碰撞

options.text_en: Flash your headlights as a warning

questions.explanation_en: Even if a pedestrian is jaywalking, you must still exercise due care and yield to avoid a collision.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 即使行人違規橫穿馬路，駕駛員仍有義務盡合理注意避免碰撞，必須讓行。

questions.stem_en: A blind pedestrian carrying a white cane is at a crosswalk. You must:
questions.stem_zhHant: 一位持白色手杖的視障行人在人行橫道處。您必須：

options.text_en: Honk to let them know you are there
options.text_zhHant: 按喇叭讓他們知道您在那裡

options.text_en: Stop and wait until they have completely crossed
options.text_zhHant: 停車等待他們完全穿過街道

options.text_en: Drive slowly past them
Wave them across

questions.explanation_en: Blind pedestrians carrying a white cane or with a guide dog always have the right-of-way. You must stop and wait until they have completely crossed.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 持白色手杖或由導盲犬陪同的視障行人始終擁有優先通行權。必須停車等待他們完全穿過街道。

questions.stem_en: A flashing 'DON'T WALK' pedestrian signal means:
questions.stem_zhHant: 閃爍的「不要走」行人信號表示：

options.text_en: Pedestrians must stop immediately wherever they are
options.text_zhHant: 行人必須立即在原地停下

options.text_en: Do not start crossing, but finish crossing if you have already started
options.text_zhHant: 不要開始橫穿，但已開始的可以繼續走完

options.text_en: Pedestrians should run to finish crossing
The signal is malfunctioning

questions.explanation_en: A flashing DON'T WALK signal means finish crossing if you have started, but do not start crossing.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 閃爍的「不要走」信號表示已開始過馬路的可以繼續走完，但不要開始新的橫穿。

questions.stem_en: Why should you NOT stop your vehicle in a crosswalk?
questions.stem_zhHant: 為什麼不應該將車輛停在人行橫道上？

options.text_en: It blocks the view for other drivers
options.text_zhHant: 它擋住了其他駕駛員的視線

options.text_en: It forces pedestrians to walk around your vehicle, putting them in danger
options.text_zhHant: 它迫使行人繞行您的車輛，使他們面臨危險

options.text_en: It damages the crosswalk markings
options.text_zhHant: 它會損壞人行橫道標記

options.text_en: It is only illegal during school hours

questions.explanation_en: Stopping in a crosswalk forces pedestrians to walk around your vehicle, putting them in danger from other traffic.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 將車輛停在人行橫道上會迫使行人繞行車輛，使他們面臨危險。

questions.stem_en: When turning at an intersection, you must:
questions.stem_zhHant: 在路口轉彎時，您必須：

options.text_en: Yield to pedestrians only if they have a WALK signal
options.text_zhHant: 僅在行人有行走信號時讓行

options.text_en: Always yield to pedestrians crossing in the crosswalk
options.text_zhHant: 始終讓正在人行橫道上通行的行人先走

options.text_en: Honk to warn pedestrians before turning
options.text_zhHant: 轉彎前按喇叭提醒行人

options.text_en: Yield to pedestrians only when turning left

questions.explanation_en: When turning at an intersection, whether right or left, you must always yield to pedestrians crossing in the crosswalk.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在路口無論左轉還是右轉，都必須讓正在人行橫道上通行的行人先走。

questions.stem_en: When you hear a siren and see flashing red lights from an emergency vehicle, you should:
questions.stem_zhHant: 當您聽到警笛並看到緊急車輛的閃爍紅燈時，您應該：

options.text_en: Speed up to get out of the way
Stop in the middle of the intersection
Pull over to the right edge of the road and stop
Continue driving at the same speed

questions.explanation_en: Pull over to the right edge of the road and stop until the emergency vehicle passes.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 靠右側路邊停車，直到緊急車輛通過。

questions.stem_en: When a school bus is flashing red lights with its stop sign arm extended, traffic in which direction(s) must stop?
questions.stem_zhHant: 當校車閃爍紅燈且伸出停車臂時，哪個方向的車輛必須停車？

options.text_en: Only traffic behind the bus
Traffic in both directions
Only traffic in front of the bus
No one needs to stop

questions.explanation_en: Traffic in BOTH directions must stop for a school bus with flashing red lights, unless on a divided highway.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 雙向車輛都必須停車，除非在有隔離帶的高速公路上。

questions.stem_en: The Move Over law requires you to do what when passing a stationary emergency vehicle with flashing lights?
questions.stem_zhHant: 移車法要求您在經過停在路邊的閃燈緊急車輛時怎麼做？

options.text_en: Stop completely and wait
Move over one lane or slow down
Flash your headlights as you pass
Honk to alert the officers

questions.explanation_en: The Move Over law requires you to move over one lane if safe, or slow down when passing stationary emergency vehicles, tow trucks, or Caltrans vehicles with flashing lights.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 移車法要求在經過停在路邊閃燈的緊急車輛、拖車或加州交通局車輛時，變道一個車道或減速通過。

questions.stem_en: At a railroad crossing with flashing lights and lowered gates, you must stop at least how many feet from the nearest rail?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在有閃燈和放下欄杆的鐵路道口，您必須在距最近鐵軌至少多少英尺處停車？

options.text_en: 5 feet
10 feet
15 feet
25 feet

questions.explanation_en: At railroad crossings, you must stop at least 15 feet from the nearest rail when lights flash or gates lower.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在鐵路道口，當燈光閃爍或欄杆放下時，必須在距最近鐵軌至少15英尺處停車。

questions.stem_en: On a divided highway with a median, when a school bus is flashing red lights:
questions.stem_zhHant: 在有中央隔離帶的公路上，當校車閃爍紅燈時：

options.text_en: All traffic in both directions must stop
options.text_zhHant: 雙向所有車輛都必須停車

options.text_en: Only traffic in the same direction as the bus must stop
options.text_zhHant: 只有與校車同向的車輛必須停車

options.text_en: No one needs to stop on a divided highway
options.text_zhHant: 在分隔公路上無人需要停車

options.text_en: Only traffic directly behind the bus must stop
options.text_zhHant: 只有校車正後方的車輛必須停車

questions.explanation_en: On a divided highway with a median, only traffic traveling in the same direction as the school bus must stop.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在有中央隔離帶的公路上，只有與校車同向行駛的車輛需要停車。

questions.stem_en: If you are in an intersection when an emergency vehicle approaches, you should:
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果緊急車輛接近時您正在路口內，您應該：

options.text_en: Stop immediately in the intersection
Proceed through the intersection first, then pull over to the right
options.text_zhHant: 先通過路口，然後靠右側停車

options.text_en: Back up to clear the intersection
Turn left to get out of the way

questions.explanation_en: If you are in an intersection when an emergency vehicle approaches, proceed through the intersection first, then pull over to the right and stop.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果緊急車輛接近時正在路口內，應先通過路口，然後靠右側停車。

questions.stem_en: When passing a bicyclist, you must give at least how many feet of clearance?
questions.stem_zhHant: 超越自行車騎行者時，必須保持至少多少英尺的安全距離？

options.text_en: 1 foot
2 feet
3 feet
5 feet

questions.explanation_en: When passing a bicyclist, you must give at least 3 feet of clearance.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 超越自行車騎行者時，必須保持至少3英尺的安全距離。

questions.stem_en: Is lane splitting by motorcyclists legal in California?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在加州，摩托車在車道之間穿行（分車道行駛）合法嗎？

options.text_en: No, it is always illegal
Yes, it is legal in California
Only on freeways
Only during heavy traffic

questions.explanation_en: Lane splitting by motorcyclists is legal in California. Be aware of motorcycles passing between lanes, especially in slow traffic.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在加州，摩托車在車道之間穿行是合法的。要注意可能在車道間穿行的摩托車。

questions.stem_en: You may drive in a marked bicycle lane when:
questions.stem_zhHant: 以下哪種情況下可以在自行車專用道內行駛？

options.text_en: There are no bicyclists present
You are within 200 feet of making a right turn
You are driving under 25 mph
It is rush hour and traffic is heavy
options.text_zhHant: 交通高峰期車流量大時

questions.explanation_en: Do not drive in a bicycle lane unless you are within 200 feet of making a right turn, entering or leaving a driveway, or parking.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 除非在200英尺內準備右轉、進出車道入口或在允許停車處停車，否則不得在自行車專用道內行駛。

questions.stem_en: Bicyclists on the road have:
questions.stem_zhHant: 道路上的自行車騎行者擁有：

options.text_en: Fewer rights than motor vehicles
The same rights and responsibilities as motor vehicle drivers
options.text_zhHant: 與機動車駕駛員同等的權利和責任

options.text_en: More rights than motor vehicles
No legal rights on public roads
options.text_zhHant: 在公共道路上沒有合法權利

questions.explanation_en: Bicyclists have the same rights and responsibilities as motor vehicle drivers.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 自行車騎行者與機動車駕駛員享有同等的權利和責任。

questions.stem_en: Before changing lanes or turning, you should always check your blind spots to avoid hitting:
questions.stem_zhHant: 變道或轉彎前應始終檢查盲區以避免撞到：

options.text_en: Only large trucks
Motorcyclists and bicyclists you may not have seen
options.text_zhHant: 可能未注意到的摩托車或自行車騎行者

options.text_en: Only vehicles in the next lane
Parked cars only

questions.explanation_en: Always check mirrors and blind spots before changing lanes or turning to avoid hitting motorcyclists or bicyclists you may not have seen.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 變道或轉彎前務必檢查後視鏡和盲區，以避免撞到可能未注意到的摩托車或自行車騎行者。
